title: NOC 14103 – Court Clerks and Related Court Services Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Court Clerks and related court services professionals play an essential role in Canada’s justice system by supporting courtroom operations, judicial records, and court administration. Classified under NOC 14103, this occupation continues to experience steady hiring across federal, provincial, and municipal courts, creating viable immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Ongoing recruitment in Canada’s justice systemStable government employmentEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement optionsEmployer-sponsored hiring in public institutions
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1410 – Office support and court services occupations

common_job_titles: Court administrator Court clerk Court clerk supervisor Court clerk-crier Court officer Criminal court clerk Judicial clerk Provincial court clerk
duties: Call courts to order and read charges
Take pleas from defendants and swear in witnesses
Prepare court files, dockets, and documents
Maintain paper and digital court records
Handle fines, fees, and trust funds
Assist the public in court offices
employment_requirements: On-the-job training for court clerks and officers
Special Constable Certification for some roles
1–2 year post-secondary diploma in office administration or related field (often required)
Court administrators may require post-secondary diplomas or university degrees in law, business, or judicial administration
Prior clerical or court experience for senior roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 5,200 new positions for Court Clerks and related occupations, while 4,900 job seekers are expected—making employer sponsorship, provincial selection, and professional immigration planning especially important for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Court Clerks and related professionals are commonly employed in:
Federal, provincial, and municipal courtsPolice servicesJudicial administration officesCorrectional and courthouse facilities
Annual earnings typically range between $37,200 and $57,800 CAD, depending on province and experience.
